2604.24396.title: Global Context or Local Detail? Adaptive Visual Grounding for Hallucination Mitigation
2604.24396.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are frequently undermined by object hallucination--generating content that contradicts visual reality--due to an over-reliance on linguistic priors. We introduce Positive-and-Negative Decoding (PND), a training-free inference framework that intervenes directly in the decoding process to enforce visual fidelity. PND is motivated by our key finding of a critical attention deficit in VLMs, where visual features are empirically under-weighted. Our framework corrects this via a dual-path contrast: The positive path amplifies salient visual evidence using multi-layer attention to encourage faithful descriptions, directly counteracting the attention deficit. Simultaneously, the negative path identifies and degrades the core object's features to create a strong counterfactual, which penalizes ungrounded, prior-dominant generation. By contrasting the model's outputs from these two perspectives at each step, PND steers generation towards text that is not just linguistically probable, but visually factual. Extensive experiments on benchmarks like POPE, MME, and CHAIR show that PND achieves state-of-the-art performance with up to 6.5% accuracy improvement, substantially reducing object hallucination while also enhancing descriptive detail--all without requiring any model retraining. The method generalizes effectively across diverse VLM architectures including LLaVA, InstructBLIP, InternVL, and Qwen-VL.
2604.24396.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）常常受到物體幻覺的影響——生成與視覺現實相矛盾的內容——這是因為過度依賴語言先驗。我們介紹了正負解碼（Positive-and-Negative Decoding, PND），這是一個無需訓練的推理框架，直接介入解碼過程以強化視覺真實性。PND 的動機來自於我們的關鍵發現，即 VLMs 中存在嚴重的注意力不足，視覺特徵在實證中被低估。我們的框架通過雙路徑對比來糾正這一點：正路徑利用多層注意力放大顯著的視覺證據，以促進忠實的描述，直接對抗注意力不足。同時，負路徑識別並削弱核心物體的特徵，以創造強烈的反事實，懲罰無根據的、以先驗為主的生成。通過在每一步從這兩個角度對比模型的輸出，PND 引導生成的文本不僅在語言上是可能的，還在視覺上是事實的。在 POPE、MME 和 CHAIR 等基準上的廣泛實驗顯示，PND 實現了最先進的性能，準確率提高了多達 6.5%，顯著減少了物體幻覺，同時增強了描述細節——所有這些都不需要任何模型重新訓練。該方法在包括 LLaVA、InstructBLIP、InternVL 和 Qwen-VL 等多種 VLM 架構中有效地進行了泛化。
2604.24396.author: Yubo Jiang et.al.
2604.24396.authors: Yubo Jiang, Xin Yang, Abudukelimu Wuerkaixi, Zheming Yuan, Xuxin Cheng, Fengying Xie, Zhiguo Jiang, Cao Liu, Ke Zeng, Haopeng Zhang
